signals.title: GBP/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + NFP Risk
signals.text: GBP/USD trades at 1.3342 as the G10 currency strength scorecard triggers a critical bilateral theme. GBP is the strongest G10 currency this week, averaging +0.791% across 4 pairs, while the USD is the weakest, averaging -0.503% across 7 pairs, resulting in a 1.294% spread. This divergence is supported by a neutral OIS carry basis (SOFR 3.68% vs SONIA 3.73% yielding a -5bp spread favoring GBP) and a positive GBP economic surprise index (CESI net +24 beats). However, the options market signals caution ahead of today's US Nonfarm Payrolls release, with the 25d risk reversal showing a moderate put skew of -0.80 and GBP/USD ATM IV at 11.0% vs HV30 at 5.6% (VRP +5.4%), indicating heavy institutional hedging against a USD-bullish NFP surprise.
signals.watch_level: Watch the US Nonfarm Payrolls release at 12:30 UTC; a strong print threatens to unwind the GBP outperformance theme back toward 1.3250.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Shock + Vol Premium
signals.text: USD/JPY plummeted 0.5% to 161.210 on suspected deliberate Japanese MoF/BoJ intervention, interrupting the dominant +270bp OIS carry advantage (SOFR 3.68% vs TONA 0.98%). JPY speculative positioning remains heavily short with COT LF net short at -115,033 contracts (28.4% long), representing a highly crowded carry trade vulnerable to rapid unwinds. The options market is aggressively pricing downside protection, with the 1M 25d risk reversal showing a moderate put skew of -1.24 and USD/JPY IV at 7.2% vs HV30 at 3.6% (VRP +3.6%), confirming that institutional desks are paying a premium to hedge JPY strength despite the massive carry deficit.
signals.watch_level: Watch for a sustained break below 160.00 to confirm a deeper intervention-driven unwind, or a rebound to test 162.00 if US NFP beats expectations.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Retail Extreme
signals.text: NZD/USD trades at 0.5687, exhibiting a multi-layer divergence. Speculators are heavily short with COT LF net short at -23,314 contracts (21.6% long), directly contradicting the hawkish RBNZ market bias which prices a rate hike for the upcoming July 8 meeting. This institutional short-squeeze risk is amplified by extreme retail sentiment, with Myfxbook community outlook showing retail traders are 88% long. This rare institutional-retail divergence, combined with a negative real policy rate of -0.83% (cb_rate 2.25% minus inflation expectations 3.08%), sets up a high-velocity short-squeeze if the RBNZ delivers a hawkish hike.
signals.watch_level: Confirm the bullish squeeze on a break above 0.5750, or invalidation if the RBNZ holds rates unchanged on July 8.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Retail Extreme
signals.text: USD/CAD trades at 1.4199, showing a significant cross-asset correlation break against WTI Oil. The USD/CAD vs WTI correlation has collapsed to -0.871 vs its 252-day norm of -0.033 (z = -1.94σ), indicating that the pair is trading almost exclusively on USD strength and domestic CAD weakness (CESI net -6 misses) rather than energy market drivers. Speculators are heavily short CAD with COT LF net short at -87,096 (20.6% long), while retail traders have reached an extreme 93% short USD/CAD. This extreme retail positioning suggests a high risk of a contrarian squeeze higher if US NFP data supports the Fed's +150bp OIS carry advantage.
signals.watch_level: Watch for a break above 1.4250 to trigger retail capitulation, or a drop below 1.4100 to signal a return to oil-driven correlation norms.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Vol Complacency + Carry Advantage
signals.text: AUD/USD trades at 0.6905, consolidating ahead of US NFP. The pair exhibits unusual volatility complacency with AUD/USD 1M IV at 3.1% vs HV30 at 6.7%, resulting in a negative Vol Risk Premium (VRP) of -3.6%. This thin options pricing suggests the market is underpricing AUD risk despite a supportive -67bp OIS carry premium favoring AUD (SOFR 3.68% vs AONIA 4.35%) and a positive real policy rate of +0.39%. Speculators remain net long AUD (COT LF net +29,035), indicating institutional backing that contrasts with the cheapness of options protection.
signals.watch_level: Watch for a volatility expansion and test of 0.6980 if US NFP misses, or a drop to 0.6820 if the US dollar strengthens.
